playbook.intent: Take an software architect from the trigger condition described in this playbook to the documented outcome, with every decision gate met in writing.
playbook.scope: Done = an architecture escalation during an incident is resolved without short-circuiting the IC: architect provides options + trade-offs in writing, IC chooses, decision is logged and links into the postmortem; no irreversible decisions are made on a phone call without a 2-line written summary.

success-criteria.criterion: On-call architecture escalation handling ships with written success criteria met and evidence attached
Baseline → post-change metric delta recorded against the relevant quality attribute
Rollback path rehearsed at least once and documented in the runbook
Decision doc (continue / iterate / revert) signed off by named owner

stage.name: Frame
stage.intent: Name the problem in writing and set explicit success criteria.

tasks.task: Write a one-paragraph problem statement tied to a quality attribute or user outcome
List concrete success criteria (measurable, observable, time-bounded)
Identify the smallest credible scope that still moves the needle

stage.decision-gate: Advance only after a peer can restate the problem in their own words and agree the criteria are testable.

stage.name: Assess
stage.intent: Inventory current state vs target with evidence, not opinion.

tasks.task: Run a structured assessment of the affected system (code, tests, docs, ops surface)
Capture baseline metrics that the work will need to beat
List the top 5 risks and one mitigation per risk

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when baselines are recorded and risks have owners.

stage.name: Plan
stage.intent: Decompose into a sequenced backlog with explicit decision points.

tasks.task: Break the work into tasks of ≤1 day each, with dependencies named
Mark reversible vs irreversible (one-way-door) decisions
Define rollback / kill conditions for each major step

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when every task has a definition-of-done and the plan fits on one page.

stage.name: Pilot
stage.intent: Prove the approach on a small, reversible slice before committing the full budget.

tasks.task: Pick the lowest-blast-radius slice that exercises the riskiest assumption
Run the slice end-to-end, including verification and rollback rehearsal
Write up what changed vs the plan and update the rollback playbook

stage.decision-gate: Advance only if the pilot meets its success criteria; otherwise re-plan or kill.

stage.name: Execute
stage.intent: Ship the change behind safe deployment controls.

tasks.task: Implement the plan in small, individually shippable steps
Keep all changes behind feature flags or branch-by-abstraction where reversible
Update tests + docs in the same change set, never as a follow-up

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when CI is green, the change is observable in staging, and rollback was rehearsed at least once.

stage.name: Verify
stage.intent: Prove the change cleared the success criteria with evidence.

tasks.task: Run targeted tests + smoke on the most-trafficked surfaces
Compare post-change metrics against the baseline captured earlier
Capture user / operator feedback within the first 48 hours

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when metrics meet the criteria and no regression alerts are open.

stage.name: Roll out
stage.intent: Expand the change from pilot scope to full target audience.

tasks.task: Increase rollout percentage in measured steps with explicit pause points
Watch SLO / error budgets on each step; halt if any breach
Communicate progress + rollback option to all affected stakeholders

stage.decision-gate: Advance only after 100% rollout has been stable for the agreed soak period.

stage.name: Close
stage.intent: Lock in the gain: documentation, learnings, and a single decision artifact.

tasks.task: Write the decision / outcome doc (continue / iterate / revert) with evidence trail
Update ADRs, runbooks, and pattern memory with what changed
Schedule the next review checkpoint or archive the workstream

stage.decision-gate: Done when the decision doc is single-link shareable and the team can name the next checkpoint.

extra.value: [Skipping the Frame stage and starting work without written success criteria, 'Treating the rollback as a doc, not a rehearsed action', Cutting the Close / decision-doc stage when the work feels obvious — that is when memory rots fastest]

['Can a peer restate the problem, criteria, and decision rule without me in the room?', 'Are the baseline metrics recorded somewhere I can grep, not just in chat?', 'Did we actually rehearse the rollback, or did we just talk about it?']

{human: faion get-content on-call-architecture-escalation-handling --format md, agent: faion get-content on-call-architecture-escalation-handling --format context, json: faion get-content on-call-architecture-escalation-handling --format json}
[{version: 1.0.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: Initial v2 draft generated from F-059-063 chunk-06 for role-software-architect (atomic angle).}]
